_comment: Per-tier baseline parameters (Table 1, updated). mu_n is the BASELINE EFFECTIVE compute rate (TFLOPS), already accounting for realistic achievable throughput. delta_n removed: memory drift now uses m_n^r = m_n - q_mem directly. Kalman process noise and sensor noise are both sigma_v^2.

effective_utilization._comment: DEPRECATED (kept at 1.0 for compatibility). mu_n in tiers below is already effective.

thermal._comment: sensor_noise_var and process_noise_var both correspond to sigma_v^2 (paper Eq.11 uses same variance for both).
thermal._T_W_comment: Fixed thermal discretization step T_W (seconds), decoupled from control window W. Defines zeta_n = 1 - exp(-T_W/tau_n). Physics ticks at this rate regardless of W_sec.
